steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The researchers argue 'invasive' and 'native' describe temporary circumstances because climate change will shift where animals can live. We need the choice that correctly applies this logic to Maryland's flathead catfish.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'flathead catfish are exempt from the temporary-circumstances principle': The researchers' argument applies to all species; no exception is made for any particular one. ✗
  • Choice B — 'past presence makes the label inappropriate now': This reverses the timeline — the argument is about future climate change, not historical range. ✗
  • Choice C — 'the invasive label may be appropriate now but not in the future': This is exactly the time-bound shift the researchers predict: current conditions justify the label today, but climate change will alter those conditions. ✓
  • Choice D — 'the catfish will establish itself regardless of climate predictions': This undermines the researchers' argument, which depends on climate change as the mechanism for range shifts. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
